description:
## Problem
`src/handlers/health.ts` defines its own loose interface types for CEM entities (lines 11-41): `CemMember`, `CemEvent`, `CemSlot`, `CemCssPart`, `CemDeclaration`. These are SEPARATE from the Zod-validated types in `src/handlers/cem.ts`. Two different type systems for the same domain objects.

When `cem.ts` types evolve (adding a new CEM field), `health.ts` won't see the change. Additionally, `CemDeclaration` in `health.ts` has `tagName: string` (required) while the Zod schema in `cem.ts` has `tagName: z.string().optional()` — these are inconsistent.

## Fix
1. Extract CEM types to `src/types/cem.ts` (or export inferred Zod types from `cem.ts`)
2. Replace the manually-defined interfaces in `health.ts` with imports from the shared location
3. Decide whether `tagName` is required or optional and enforce consistently
4. Ensure `scoreCemFallback` and `scoreComponent` use the same `CemDeclaration` type the CEM handler produces

## Acceptance Criteria
- No duplicate type definitions for CEM entities across the codebase
- Types derived from a single source of truth (Zod inference preferred)
- All 131 existing tests still pass
- TypeScript type-check passes clean
title: Eliminate duplicate CEM type definitions across cem.ts and health.ts

descriptionHistory.description:
## Problem
`src/handlers/health.ts` defines its own loose interface types for CEM entities (lines 11-41): `CemMember`, `CemEvent`, `CemSlot`, `CemCssPart`, `CemDeclaration`. These are SEPARATE from the Zod-validated types in `src/handlers/cem.ts`. Two different type systems for the same domain objects.

When `cem.ts` types evolve (adding a new CEM field), `health.ts` won't see the change. Additionally, `CemDeclaration` in `health.ts` has `tagName: string` (required) while the Zod schema in `cem.ts` has `tagName: z.string().optional()` — these are inconsistent.

## Fix
1. Extract CEM types to `src/types/cem.ts` (or export inferred Zod types from `cem.ts`)
2. Replace the manually-defined interfaces in `health.ts` with imports from the shared location
3. Decide whether `tagName` is required or optional and enforce consistently
4. Ensure `scoreCemFallback` and `scoreComponent` use the same `CemDeclaration` type the CEM handler produces

## Acceptance Criteria
- No duplicate type definitions for CEM entities across the codebase
- Types derived from a single source of truth (Zod inference preferred)
- All 131 existing tests still pass
- TypeScript type-check passes clean
